div.head: The Acting Secretary of State to the Ambassador in China (Johnson)The same as No. 273 to the Ambassador In Japan.
div.p: 325. According to an Associated Press report from Shanghai of October 29 the German Ambassador was stated to be in Nanking acting as a mediator and messenger carrying proposed Japanese peace terms.See Department of State, Documents on German Foreign Policy, 1918–1945, series D, vol. i, pp. 733 ff. The report states further that the German Ambassador recently spent a week in Shanghai in conference with the Japanese Ambassador and other high officials.
The Department has received information to this same general effect attributed to Suma.Japanese Counselor of Embassy in the United States.
Please telegraph urgently what you can discreetly ascertain in regard to this report and your estimate as to its significance.
